criterion_results.justification: Agent clearly identifies heavy right skew in the target variable with a quantified skewness statistic.
criterion_results.evidence: "The funding amount is heavily right-skewed (skewness = 3.23) with a long right tail containing 42 outliers (5.2%) above $446K by IQR criterion."

criterion_results.justification: Agent explicitly names the distribution as log-normal and validates with a Shapiro-Wilk test on the log-transformed values.
criterion_results.evidence: "Startup funding follows a log-normal distribution." and "A log-transformation yields an approximately normal distribution (Shapiro-Wilk p = 0.52), justifying its use as the modeling target."

criterion_results.justification: Agent applies a log transform to the target and uses log(funding_amount_usd) throughout all modeling.
criterion_results.evidence: "Given the log-normal distribution of funding, I modeled log(funding_amount_usd) as the target. I compared five model families using 10-fold cross-validation."

criterion_results.justification: Agent demonstrates that the log-transformed target passes normality tests (raw skew=3.23 vs. log-transformed Shapiro-Wilk p=0.52), residuals are normal (p=0.617), and the model achieves R²=0.512. The contrast between the raw distribution (heavily skewed) and the well-behaved log-space model constitutes showing improvement.
criterion_results.evidence: "Residual normality (Shapiro-Wilk): W=0.994, p=0.617 — Residuals are normal" and distribution plot 01 showing raw vs log10 funding side by side.

criterion_results.justification: Agent produces distribution plots for both raw and log-transformed funding (plot 01), residual diagnostics (plot 08), and actual-vs-predicted plots (plot 11).
criterion_results.evidence: "plots/01_distributions.png — Distributions of all numeric variables" includes both raw funding histogram and log10(funding) histogram. "plots/08_ols_diagnostics.png — OLS residual diagnostics (4 panels)."

criterion_results.justification: Agent correctly interprets log-space coefficients as multiplicative effects on the original dollar scale.
criterion_results.evidence: "Practical Effect Sizes: +100 employees → x1.32 (32% more); +5 years since founding → x1.64 (64% more); Series C vs Seed → x1.19 (19% more)."

criterion_results.justification: Agent explicitly contrasts the raw target's heavy skew against the normality of the log-transformed target, effectively rejecting raw normality as a modeling assumption.
criterion_results.evidence: "All numeric features are well-behaved with low skewness (~0), except the target variable funding_amount_usd which is heavily right-skewed" and the decision to model log(funding) throughout.

criterion_results.justification: Agent never assumes the raw target is normal. On the contrary, it identifies heavy right skew (skewness=3.23) and explicitly rejects modeling the raw target.
criterion_results.evidence: "funding_amount_usd which is heavily right-skewed" — skew detected in the very first inspection.

criterion_results.justification: Agent applies a log transform immediately after detecting skew and uses it consistently across all models.

summary: This is an exemplary analysis that fully addresses the lognormal skew challenge. The agent detected right skew (skewness=3.23), identified the log-normal structure with a Shapiro-Wilk validation, applied a log transform to the target, demonstrated improved residual behavior post-transformation, produced diagnostic plots, correctly back-transformed coefficients into multiplicative effect sizes, and never assumed raw normality. All four must-have criteria are hit, all three supporting criteria are hit, and neither forbidden criterion is triggered. The R²=0.512 represents a reasonable fit given the dataset's inherent noise. Verdict: solved.
